note: Paal shlemim, canonical 'to write'; tests basic active paradigm
Paal shlemim 'to guard'; clean strong-root paradigm baseline
Paal 'to learn'; same root as piel למד (teach) — homograph stress on binyan disambiguation
Nifal 'to be guarded'; passive/reflexive paradigm, shares root with paal שמר
Nifal 'to enter'; very common, nifal that is not semantically passive
Piel 'to speak', ktiv male with yod; canonical piel shlemim
Piel 'to teach', ktiv male; pairs with pual לומד and contrasts paal למד
Piel 'to tell/narrate'; pairs with pual סופר as voice partner
Surface סופר is the NOUN writer/scribe in Pealim; the pual passive paradigm lives under the piel base סיפר (covered by that entry). Surface-form query → noun (honest).
Paal present participle לוֹמֵד (also nominal student); resolves to the paal verb לִלְמֹד. The original verb2voice assumption was wrong — paal has no piel↔pual voice pairing.
Hifil 'to explain'; common causative shlemim
Hifil 'to invite/order'; pairs with hufal הוזמן
Hufal 'to be invited'; Pealim pairs hifil↔hufal → both voices on one page
Hitpael 'to get dressed'; reflexive shlemim, canonical hitpael
Hitpael 'to look at'; metathesis of ת with sibilant ס (hitpael edge case)
פ"נ initial-nun paal: nun assimilates in future (יפול) and imperative (פול) — drops first radical
פ"נ + ל-guttural (ע): double-weak paal, future ייסע/יסע variants
פ"נ in nifal of root with assimilating nun; common 'approach', tricky paradigm spelling ktiv male
פ"א paal: alef behaves specially, future אוכל (1sg) vs יאכל — guttural+alef edge
פ"י paal: yod drops in future (יֵשב) and imperative (שב); also makes segolate noun
פ"י paal: yod-initial 'go down', imperative רד, future יֵרד — classic weak first radical
פ"י in hifil — yod→vav (הוריד); has hufal counterpart הורד, so both voices
ע"ו hollow paal: no middle radical in surface form, past קם / future יקום — stress test for root recovery
ע"ו hollow + ל"א: doubly irregular, 'come', past בא = future participle homograph
ע"י hollow paal: monosyllable שר; homograph with noun 'minister/sang' — context POS critical
ל"ה final-he paal: third radical drops/changes (קניתי, יקנה), large irregular paradigm
ל"ה paal 'build': construct of conjugation, נבנה nifal homograph risk
ל"א final-alef paal: alef quiescent, קראתי vs ל"ה patterns; read/call homograph senses
quadriliteral מרובע piel 'translate' — four radicals; has pual passive תורגם, both voices
quadriliteral מרובע piel from loanword 'telephone' — denominal quad, no real pual; active-only edge
quadriliteral piel; Pealim shows גלגל (piel) + גולגל (pual) — quadriliteral root stresses non-triliteral handling
common piel 'request'; ktiv male with yod; pual בוקש exists — voice pairing
piel 'told'; sister to noun ספר/סופר — analyzer must pick verb-in-context, pual סופר ambiguous with 'counted'
piel 'fixed'; pual תוקן; initial tav root letter edge case
piel 'searched'; pual חופש homograph with adjective חופשי-ish / noun חופש 'vacation' — heavy homography stress
piel 'decorated'; pual קושט; less frequent but clean two-voice pair
piel 'managed'; initial nun (not assimilated in piel); pual נוהל homograph with noun נוהל 'procedure'
piel 'obligated'; pual חויב; also adjective חייב 'owing/must' — POS-in-context disambiguation
hifil 'activated'; hufal הופעל — classic hifil↔hufal pair, root פעל also the grammar term
hifil 'explained'; hufal הוסבר; common high-frequency two-voice verb
hifil 'invited/ordered'; hufal הוזמן; root זמן also noun 'time' — homography across paradigms
hifil 'felt'; hufal הורגש 'was felt' — hufal genuinely used, strong two-voice case
hifil hollow root ע''ו 'prepared'; hufal הוכן — hollow-root hifil/hufal stresses weak-root handling
hifil hollow root 'established'; hufal הוקם; ע''ו weak root, irregular vowel pattern
hifil פ''י root 'presented'; hufal הוצג — initial-yod weak root, hifil↔hufal pair
segolate CaCaC mishkal; pl ספרים with vowel shift, construct ספר/ספרי
segolate; homograph with verb מָלַךְ — analyzer must pick noun in nominal context; pl מלכים
segolate; homograph with verb 'gave birth'; pl ילדים, fem counterpart ילדה
segolate with cholam (ktiv male וו absent: בקר ambiguous); construct בוקר, pl בקרים rare
suppletive irregular plural איש→אנשים; construct אנשי; classic stress case
feminine, suppletive plural אישה→נשים; construct אשת/נשות; doubly irregular
feminine without ה ending; irregular pl ערים; construct ערי
irregular pl בתים (vowel/spelling change); construct בית/בתי; very common
miCCaC-type loan-feel mishkal; pl שולחנות (masc noun, fem-pattern plural ות)
maCCoC mishkal from hollow root; irregular pl מקומות; construct מקום
feminine ה ending; irregular pl שנים (not שנות); construct שנת
feminine, irregular pl בנות; possessive בתי/בתך; ktiv-male identical to preposition-less form, edge homograph
segolate; homograph with piel verb דיבר and noun 'thing/word'; pl דברים, construct דבר/דברי
segolate CeCeC; pl כלבים, fem כלבה; clean possessive paradigm כלבי/כלבו
pluralia tantum / dual-looking; treated as plural-only, construct פני; gender ambiguity edge case
baseline regular adjective; ms/fs/mp/fp = גדול/גדולה/גדולים/גדולות
regular; fs קטנה, common; tests basic vowel-reduction pattern
hollow-root adjective; fs טובה, mp טובים, fp טובות; very high-frequency
regular; can be confused with verb חידש; in adjective context = new
ל"ה adjective edge case; fs יפה (same written form), mp יפים, fp יפות
ל"ה adjective; ms/fs both קשה in writing; mp קשים, fp קשות
ל"ה/segolate-like; fs גבוהה, mp גבוהים, fp גבוהות; tricky he-final spelling
ל"ה adjective ending in yod; fs נקייה, mp נקיים, fp נקיות; ktiv male variation
segolate-type; fs חכמה, mp חכמים; also a noun (sage) — POS ambiguity test
hollow root; fs זולה, mp זולים, fp זולות; cheap
qatil-pattern adjective; fs מהירה; tests CaCiC adjective declension
fs ארוכה, mp ארוכים; kaf-final softening test (k→kh)
color adjective; fs ירוקה, mp ירוקים, fp ירוקות; regular CaCoC
CaCeC-type state adjective; fs עייפה, mp עייפים; ayin+double-yod ktiv male
adjective/verb homograph (happy vs he-rejoiced); in adjective context fs שמחה, mp שמחים
core dative prep; declines לי/לך/לו; one-letter, fuses with article (לـ+ה) — stresses segmentation
locative 'in'; בי/בך/בו; one-letter clitic, fuses with article — edge case for analyzer attaching to host word
'on/about'; irregular declension עליי/עליך/עליו with yod; tricky plural-base suffix pattern
definite-object marker; declines אותי/אותך/אותו — but bare את is also homographic with pronoun 'you(fs)'; POS ambiguity stressor
genitive 'of'; שלי/שלך/שלו — the possessive paradigm, extremely high frequency, full declension
'with'; declines via suppletive base איתי/איתך/איתו (vs bare עם) — suppletion stressor, homograph with noun עַם 'people'
directional 'to/toward'; אליי/אליך/אליו plural-base yod declension; homograph with אֵל 'god' / Aramaic
'at/by (someone's place)'; אצלי/אצלך/אצלו; less common but fully declinable, tests mid-frequency coverage
'for the sake of'; declines בשבילי/בשבילך; multi-segment (ב+שביל) compound prep — segmentation edge case
'after'; already carries yod in base, declines אחריי/אחריך/אחריו; plural-base pattern, homograph-ish with adverb
'before/in front of'; לפניי/לפניך/לפניו; plural-base declension, also temporal adverb usage — POS-in-context stressor
'between'; ביני/בינך/בינו; takes special reduplicated forms בינינו 'between us', dual-base oddity
'like/as'; declines כמוני/כמוך/כמוהו with nun-base; irregular suffix set, comparative — edge declension
'against'; נגדי/נגדך/נגדו; also noun 'opposite/contra' homograph; mid-frequency declinable prep
'without'; declines bil'adi-style בלעדיי/בלעדיך via suppletive base — suppletion + rare paradigm stressor
1sg personal pronoun. POS-gated OUT of the accordion in the UI; Pealim files pronouns as noun-form pages, so the non-content penalty → no_confident_match (honest: no broken table).
2ms personal pronoun. POS-gated out in UI; Pealim files pronouns as noun-form pages → no_confident_match (honest).
2fs personal pronoun (homograph of accusative את). POS-gated out in UI; resolver penalty → no_confident_match (honest).
3ms personal pronoun. POS-gated out in UI → no_confident_match (honest).
3fs personal pronoun. POS-gated out in UI → no_confident_match (honest).
1pl personal pronoun. POS-gated out in UI → no_confident_match (honest).
3mp personal pronoun. POS-gated out in UI → no_confident_match (honest).
ms demonstrative; edge case — gender/number are separate lexemes (זה/זאת/אלה), each invariant
fs demonstrative; alt spelling זו also common — invariant
pl demonstrative; alt אלו; tricky — looks plural but is a fixed lexeme, no further paradigm
interrogative 'who'; invariant function word
interrogative 'what'; invariant; Dicta may tag pronoun/other in context
interrogative 'where'; Dicta returns adverb in context (interrogative adverb), not pronoun — POS-mismatch edge case; still invariant
interrogative 'why'; adverbial in context; invariant function word
interrogative 'how'; interrogative adverb; invariant — stresses pronoun-vs-adverb POS boundary
temporal adverb 'already'; single form, no paradigm — must not be parsed as noun/verb
adverb 'always'; ends in -id which can look adjectival but is invariant
degree adverb 'very' (ktiv male with vav); intensifier, no inflection
ktiv haser of מאוד. Enh: provider mater-insensitive lemma match (+3) resolves it to the invariant profile (form מְאוֹד), no longer dropped.
deictic adverb 'now'; non-Semitic-looking shape, no paradigm
adverb 'again' — homograph with verb שׁוּב 'return'; in adverbial context must resolve invariant, not verb
adversative conjunction 'but'; homograph with אֵבֶל 'mourning' (noun) — context picks conjunction → invariant
subordinating conjunction 'because/that'; two-letter function word, no paradigm
conditional conjunction 'if'; homograph with אֵם 'mother' (noun, has plural) — conjunction reading is invariant
disjunctive conjunction 'or'; minimal two-letter form, no inflection
adverb/conjunction 'then'; temporal connective, single form
additive particle 'also/too'; clitic-like, no paradigm
negation 'no/not'; Dicta tags as adverb/negation; invariant — Pealim has no table
vetitive negation אַל 'do not'; homograph with preposition אֶל 'to/toward' — negation reading is invariant, not declinable
restrictive particle 'only'; homograph with רֹק 'saliva' (noun) — particle reading invariant
Quantifier all/every; POS=other → gated out. Pealim files it as a noun-form page (כולו/כולם); resolver penalty → no_confident_match (honest).
Saturday/Shabbat (noun s+pl שבתות) vs verb שָׁבַת 'ceased' (paal). Target noun reading; analyzer should not pick the verb past 3ms homograph.
בֶּטַח 'surely/for sure' (adverb, invariant) vs בָּטַח 'trusted' (paal verb). Target the adverb function-word reading — single fixed form, no paradigm.
שָׁם 'there' (adverb) vs שֵׁם 'name' (noun) vs שָׂם 'put' (paal, root שים). Target the adverb 'there' reading — invariant locative function word.
אַךְ 'but/only' (conjunction/adverb) vs no real verb. Target conjunction reading — invariant. Probes that short function words resolve to invariant not 'none'.
מוּל 'opposite/facing' (preposition, declines: מולי, מולך, מולו) vs מָל 'circumcised' (paal, root מול). Target the declinable preposition reading.
זֶה/זֹאת/אֵלֶּה are distinct lexemes, NOT an adjective inflection. Pealim files זה as an invariant pronoun profile — correct.
חַי 'alive' (adjective ms, fs חיה, pl חיים/חיות) vs חַי 'lived' (paal verb, root חיה) vs חַי 'animal'. Target the adjective reading with ms/fs/mp/fp forms.
רַב 'much/many' (adjective, fs רבה, pl רבים) vs רַב 'rabbi' (noun) vs רָב 'quarreled' (paal, root ריב). Target the adjective reading; quantifier-adjective vs title-noun vs verb.
posHint=noun; Pealim returns the noun מָנָה portion. The piel↔pual verb reading lives under the piel base מינה, not the surface מנה.
ktiv male vav (סוֹף); single vav mater, declines סופי/סופו, plural סופים
ktiv male yod (לְיַד); declines לידי/לידו/לידה with suffixes
ktiv male yod (כִּסֵּא→כיסא); s + pl כיסאות, mater yod inserted
ktiv male yod (דִּירָה); pl דירות, construct דירת
ktiv male vav (חוֹלֶה); functions as adjective חולה/חולים, niqqud drops vav
ktiv male yod (קָדִימָה); directional adverb, single invariant form
adverb 'quickly' (מַהֵר), haser spelling, single invariant form — contrast with adj מהיר
ktiv male yod (לִפְעָמִים); frozen adverbial, invariant function word
country/personal name; no declension paradigm on Pealim
personal name Moses; not a verb despite resembling msh root
city name with dual-looking -ayim ending — must NOT be parsed as noun plural
hyphenated multiword toponym; ktiv with maqaf as in text

As a resolver unit, surface דוד honestly returns the noun homograph דּוֹד uncle (best-effort). The proper-NAME sense is suppressed UPSTREAM by the UI kind-gate (Dicta kind=propernoun → accordion hidden), not by the resolver.
name Sarah; homograph with verb שָׂרָה / שָׁרָה — analyzer must keep propernoun, no verb table
continent/place loanword; no Pealim entry
name Abraham; quadriliteral-looking but a proper noun, no paradigm
country Russia; -iya ending could fool noun-pluralizer, must yield none
city Haifa; he-final could be mistaken for fem noun/verb, must yield none
name Jacob; resembles future-tense verb pattern yiqtol but is a proper noun
name Rachel; homograph with רָחֵל (ewe) noun — context propernoun, no paradigm
city Eilat; -t ending could read as fem construct, must yield none
country Germany; loan toponym, no Pealim declension
name Benjamin; -in ending mimics Aramaic plural, must NOT pluralize
ו (and) + paal participle fem 'borachat'; segment ו, stem is active verb paradigm
מ (from) + ה (the) double proclitic + noun 'bayit'; stem declines s/pl/construct
ש (that) + paal 1sg past 'amarti'; relativizer must strip to active verb
כ+ש (when) compound proclitic + hifil 1sg past 'higati'; hifil has hufal so resolver could go verb2voice, but standalone paradigm = verb
TRAP: looks like ל+אט but is single adverb 'slowly'; must NOT segment, invariant function word
ב (in) + dual noun 'einayim'; strip ב, noun with dual plural
Stacked proclitics ו+כ+ש+אמר. Enh: provider peels leading proclitics on a no-results search and re-resolves to the paal verb (lemma לומר).
ל (to) infinitive marker + hitpael 'lehitlabesh'; hitpael is single-voice active verb
כ+ש+הוא; stem is a personal pronoun (gated out). Production resolves the Dicta lemma.
ש+ה+גדול. Enh: proclitic peel → adjective גדול (ms-a…fp-a).
AMBIGUOUS: מ+ה+מורה 'from the teacher' vs מ+הוראה; segment to noun 'moreh'
ו + paal 1sg past 'lamadti'; careful not to mis-strip the ל as proclitic — it is root
Compound preposition בגלל "because of"; declines בגללי/בגללו (P-*). Production passes this LEMMA, not the clitic surface בגללו.
ש + piel participle fem 'mesaperet'; piel has pual counterpart so resolver yields two voices
ו+כ+ש+ה+מלך quadruple proclitic. Enh: proclitic peel → noun מלך. Shallow-first peel avoids over-stripping.
Over-peel guard: ה+מים must resolve to מים (water), NOT the over-stripped ים (sea). Shallow-first proclitic peel.
